---
title: Registering a ZK Chain
---

::callout{icon="i-heroicons-exclamation-triangle" color="amber"}
For security reasons while the ecosystem matures, registration is currently whitelisted by the governance. In the future registering can become fully permissionless.
::

## What does registering a ZK chain mean?

Registering a ZK chain on the Mainnet or Testnet is the process of registering a ZK chain to the ZKsync ecosystem, after which you can deploy it.

Registration involves making some calls to existing contracts and creating a set of L1 contracts for that ZK chain in a way that it becomes part of
the ZK Router `Bridgehub`.

::callout{icon="i-heroicons-light-bulb"}
If a ZK chain is not deployed to the ZK Router, it cannot be added there in the future. It must be deployed as part of the ecosystem.
::

## Required actions

`Bridgehub` is the contract that can be considered an ecosystem hub, so registering a chain starts with it.

- If the ZK chain uses a custom base token, the token has to be registered on the `Bridgehub` before the chain registration.
- The ZK chain itself is being registered via `createNewChain` call.

The second stage is an `initializeChainGovernance` call to the ZK Router to register the L2 bridge address on L1. An address of the L2 bridge can be
obtained by either deploying it (preferred option from the security perspective) or by calculating the address via `create2`.

::callout{icon="i-heroicons-light-bulb"}
Governance is the owner of both `Bridgehub` and ZK Router, so all these calls have to be done by the governance.
::

## Preparing a calldata

You need to prepare the right calldata to schedule a governance operation that perform the calls described above. This calldata has to be approved by
the security council before the operation can be executed.

A script has to be used to prepare the calldata, it is configured via `l1-contracts/script-config/prepare-registration-calldata.toml` file, which has
to be created manually.

### Example config for `prepare-registration-calldata.toml`

The example config is stored here: `l1-contracts/deploy-script-config-template/config-prepare-registration-calldata.toml`. It might be updated over
time, so its better to refer to the values from GitHub, currently it looks like:

```bash
[deployed_addresses]
state_transition_proxy_addr = "0x635853efd1d388f597feb9fe06e666efda397911"
erc20_bridge_proxy_addr = "0x147CDc5DD347bA141545Ad08fce748484Ed7fDbA"


[chain]
chain_id = 123
era_chain_id = 9
admin = "0x70997970C51812dc3A010C7d01b50e0d17dc79C8"
diamond_cut_data = ""
bridgehub_create_new_chain_salt = 0
base_token_addr = "0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000001"
```

Explanations of some fields:

- `erc20_bridge_proxy_addr` - is not going to be used by the chain itself, but it’s required to create the L2 bridge
- `diamond_cut_data` - can be generated using .ts scripts, but it’s easier to find it in `etc/env/ecosystems/sepolia.yaml`
- `bridgehub_create_new_chain_salt` - bridgehub accepts a salt in the `createNewChain` function, that’s where it’s used. Also, it is reused as a salt
for the governance ops for convenience. Salt value can be left by default as 0.

### Prepare ZK chain registration calldata

To determine the correct address of the L2 bridge before deploying it, you have to run the script impersonating the address that will be deploying
the L2 bridge:

```bash
forge script --rpc-url $MAINNET --sender $SENDER PrepareZKChainRegistrationCalldata.s.sol
```

The output is in the `l1-contracts/script-out/output-prepare-registration-calldata.toml`, it contains 4 fields:

- `scheduleCalldataStageOne` - calldata that schedules a governance operation performing the first stage.
- `executeCalldataStageOne` - calldata that executes a governance operation performing the first stage.
- `scheduleCalldataStageTwo` - calldata that schedules a governance operation performing the second stage.
- `executeCalldataStageTwo` - calldata that executes a governance operation performing the second stage.

### Further steps

There are some calls that have to be done by the chain admin:

- Add validators
- Set pubdata sending mode
- Set base token gas price multiplier (if using custom base token)

### Finalize registration

::callout{icon="i-heroicons-exclamation-triangle" color="amber"}
For security reasons while the ecosystem matures, registration is currently whitelisted by the governance. In the future registering can become fully permissionless.
::

To register a new chain on mainnet (or testnet), you will need to prepare 3 things as the chain admin:

- Your chain id (make sure that it is unique and doesn’t match anything in i.e. [chainlist](https://chainlist.org/))
- Configuration for the STM (what’s the base token, etc)
- Address for the admin of the ZK chain

You can find all the contract addresses for both mainnet and testnet in the [public docs](/build/developer-reference/addresses/zk-chain-addresses) or
in `etc/env/ecosystems/sepolia.yaml` (ecosystem files may be updated more frequently than the docs).

Finally contact ZK Foundation or Matter Labs to register your ZK chain on the ZK Router ecosystem via the governance operation.
